title: Lets discuss anime that influenced Eva and the ways it did
display_title: Lets discuss anime that influenced Eva and the ways it did
wikitext:
[]: Lets discuss anime that influenced Eva and the ways it did             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 17, 2006 3:05 am as the title says, let's talk about what anime helped influence Eva and to what effect

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 17, 2006 7:22 am Ideon had the giant robot that was more effective, the younger the pilots; and the conjoined consciousness behind it that brought about the end of everything. That's one source which is, I believe, explicitly acknowledged.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Dec 17, 2006 7:58 am You have to give a little recognition to Nausicaa. It was Anno's first anime he was a key animator for and that whole Giant Warriors destroying the earth thing sounds a bit familiar. (Although I think he drew more ideas for Nadia from working on that film than Eva.) Also I would give a nod to Macross as he worked with that anime a lot when he was young.

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Sun Dec 17, 2006 8:53 pm Theres a fine line between being influenced by somthing and ripping it off. Example-RAXEPHONE I hated it.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 17, 2006 10:42 pm Let's not turn this into another "HEY GUYS RAHXEPHON IS AN EVA RIPOFF, LET'S NOW STATE OTHER COMPLETELY OBVIOUS FACTS!!!" thread.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Mon Dec 18, 2006 5:05 am RahXephon isn't an Eva rip off, sure there are minor similarities but overall the story was it's own. You could see it was influenced by parts of Eva though just as Eva was influenced by other series before it. Anyway to actually bring this back on topic I actually want to see Ideon but I've noticed it doesn't seem to be available where I am, i'm guessing it's not licensed?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Dec 18, 2006 2:14 pm not licensed, see animesuki for the shin-getter fansubs.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Feb 02, 2007 12:51 pm BTW, "Be Invoked" has been released (the 2nd Ideon movie, that replaces the last 5 episodes of the series) by shin-getter. mmm yummy I just finished watching it, didn't have much time over the last week to watch anime (or sleep), Twilight Princess takes highest priority.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Feb 02, 2007 1:07 pm WTF? I watched the RAW 2 days ago thinking Shingetter wouldn't release subs in the near future. Well, I'll watch again... couldn't understand much what was the Ide wanted. I read interviews in which Anno also stated Go Nagai's "Devilman", Uchuu Senkan Yamato and Gundam.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Fri Feb 02, 2007 1:09 pm Yeah Rahxephon wasn't a complete ripoff, but there was the constant repeted imagery (water drip) and instrumentalist anyone. Too many surface level similarities to get past eaisly. From what I've read Ideon approaches the theme from a different angle than NGE. [Spoiler] NGE: Individuality = good complimentation = bad. In Ideon it's reversed. [/spoiler]

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Feb 02, 2007 3:06 pm Your spoiler is what I heard about it, too. That's the main reason I don't want to see it, the thought that things go smoothly for what you pointed out is rather distrubing, IMO. :( (Hence why I like Eva, espeically EoTV/EoE so much.)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Feb 02, 2007 3:11 pm **spoliers, maybe*** Things don't go smoothly, everyone dies horrible deaths, complimentation is a completely different thing than in Eva, since the Ide is already a collection of the souls of a previous civilization, and the ending of Be Invoked is just like the EoTV ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Feb 02, 2007 3:19 pm That's what I mean, the Instrumentality-like occurance goes smoothly in that, which is why I don't think I'd want to see it. the ending of Be Invoked is just like the EoTV ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Feb 02, 2007 3:32 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: That's what I mean, the Instrumentality-like occurance goes smoothly in that, which is why I don't think I'd want to see it.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Feb 02, 2007 4:54 pm Call me naive, but I've always thought EVA was what influenced other anime's, not the other way around. It's just so unique that nothing really comes close in my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Feb 02, 2007 4:58 pm TriLink12 wrote: Call me naive, but I've always thought EVA was what influenced other anime's, not the other way around.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Feb 02, 2007 5:06 pm Mr. Tines wrote: TriLink12 wrote: Call me naive, but I've always thought EVA was what influenced other anime's, not the other way around.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sat Feb 03, 2007 2:14 am I know that Anno was somewhat close to the production of Rahxephon, he even gave an interview about it or something, IRC, in a Rah artbook. Anyone has more info on this?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 03, 2007 8:52 am http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=597

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sat Feb 03, 2007 12:25 pm Ornette wrote:
